text: The robotic procedure begins with the placement of ports, marked in red for robotic and green for assistant. The surgeon opens the peritoneum at the Douglas pouch, followed by dissection of the rectovaginal space and anterior rectum. A lateral dissection on the left side of the rectum reveals the muscle defect of the pelvic floor. The global view shows the fully dissected muscle defect. A peritoneal incision is made at the sacral promontory, and the retrorectal space is dissected to reach the pelvic floor, joining both dissections.

The surgeon performs a digital rectal examination, revealing the perineal hernia and the rectum. A biological mesh is placed over the muscle defect, and its proximal end is sutured to the sacral promontory in a posterior rectopexy fashion. Interrupted Vicryl IIO sutures are used to secure the mesh to the lateral rectal wall and the pelvic floor, with additional sutures fixing the mesh to the promontory.
The surgeon closes the peritoneum using two running barbed sutures of PDS-III-O, completing the final step of the hernia repair procedure.
